seo.title: FAQ — Automation Sprint & Blueprint
seo.description: Answers on scope, AI spend caps, timelines, fit, and what Bekur does not do. Honest disqualifiers for professional service firms.

hero.headline: Questions operators ask before they commit

items.question: What is an Automation Sprint?
items.answer: A fixed-scope engagement to ship one production workflow in 3–6 weeks. Logic Mapping comes first, then build, then partner approval and go-live.

items.question: Can you automate multiple workflows in one sprint?
items.answer: No. One workflow per sprint keeps scope honest and adoption high. Additional workflows mean a new sprint or a signed change order.

items.question: How do you cap AI spend?
items.answer: We agree a hard monthly AI spend limit in writing before build. Architecture and monitoring are designed to stay inside that cap.

items.question: Do I need Blueprint before a sprint?
items.answer: Not always, but we recommend it when logic is messy or the quote needs certainty. Blueprint maps the workflow and fixes sprint price before production code.

items.question: Will you replace our Clio / CRM / practice management system?
items.answer: No. We build into your existing stack. Full SaaS replacement is out of scope.

items.question: When will we see ROI?
items.answer: We target a measurable outcome within 90 days of go-live. Blueprint and Logic Mapping define what we measure.

items.question: Who actually does the work?
items.answer: Senior engineers and operators. We don't put juniors on client sprints.

items.question: Do you provide SOC 2 or HIPAA-certified pipelines?
items.answer: No. If you require certifications we cannot credibly deliver, we're not the right partner.

items.question: Can our IT team run the purchase?
items.answer: The managing partner or founder needs to own the engagement. Committee-only buying is a poor fit for sprints.

items.question: Where are you based? Do you work globally?
items.answer: We're in Addis Ababa and serve US, UK, Canada, and Australia. Delivery is remote; voice is operator-first, not geography-led.
